name: Common Platform Enumeration
owner: NIST (National Vulnerability Database) — originally MITRE, transferred to NIST in 2014
description: CPE is a structured naming scheme for IT systems, software, and packages. Each CPE name encodes vendor, product, version, update, edition, language, software edition, target software, target hardware, and other attributes (CPE 2.3 well-formed name or URI form). CPE names appear in CVE entries as the affected-product identifiers, in NVD product enumeration, and in scanning/inventory tools. Originally a MITRE project (CPE 2.2); maintenance transferred to NIST/NVD circa 2014 with the 2.3 specification.
relationToCloudSecurity: CPE is the substrate for vulnerability-to-product mapping in cloud asset inventory and vulnerability management. Container images, OS packages, and cloud-managed services are commonly tagged with CPE names so CVE feeds can be matched against installed inventory.
relationToAISecurity: CPE has no AI-specific extensions. AI models/datasets are not currently first-class entities in CPE.

license.notes: CPE is maintained by NIST/NVD; works of NIST are in the US public domain (17 USC 105). The legacy MITRE CPE 2.2 spec is on the MITRE site under their terms.

lifecycle.notes: CPE 2.3 is the current specification (NIST IR 7695, 7696, 7697, 7698). Filed under nvd.nist.gov per the DNS-canonical rule — NVD is the publishing organization. Previously filed under mitre.org for historical continuity (CPE 2.2 was a MITRE project, transferred to NIST in 2014). See SecID redirect at secid:reference/mitre.org/cpe -> secid:reference/nvd.nist.gov/cpe.

current_extraction.notes: Structured the 11 CPE 2.3 WFN attributes into cpe-2.3-attributes.{csv,json}. Columns: order (1-11 per URI binding), attribute_name, is_extended (true for sw_edition/target_sw/target_hw/other), is_deprecated (true for edition), description (pulled from Section 5.3.3.N), allowed_values (for `part`: a/o/h).

extraction_history.notes: README-only stub. CPE dictionary is consumed dynamically from NVD by tools that need it; no local snapshot maintained here.

notes: Reference entry only. CPE is foundational to CVE/NVD workflows; for current data pull from NVD.
